title: Five-hole
summary: The five-hole is an ice hockey term for the space between a goaltender's legs. The name and its first recorded usage was in 1972 by legendary goalie Jacques Plante in his book, On Goaltending. The phrases through the five-hole and gone five-hole are used when a player scores by shooting the puck into the goal between the goaltender's legs. The term is also used in basketball, association football, field hockey, and lacrosse. In baseball, the term is used to describe a wild pitch between the catcher's legs.
